With deep experience across multi-cloud platforms and enterprise infrastructure, he specializes in designing and operating resilient, production-grade cloud-native infrastructure environments that power modern applications at scale.  Alex actively explores emerging technologies in DevSecOps, automation, and cloud ecosystem while championing best practices in reliability and cybersecurity. He occasionally blogs about his thoughts on cloud-native infrastructure.

person.name: Adrian Damm
person.bio: As a Principal Cloud Architect, I specialize in guiding cloud transformation projects and training professionals in Kubernetes, Helm, and other cloud-native tools. I focus on helping teams package, deploy, and orchestrate microservices at scale using cutting-edge technologies. I'm passionate about sharing my knowledge and helping others build resilient, scalable systems.

person.name: Viktor Khashchevyy
person.bio: I was born in Ukraine and have spent most of my life in Italy’s Veneto region. I started my career as a NetAdmin at an ISP, and over time, my professional growth led me into SRE and DevOps roles. Learning Linux literally changed everything: it ignited my passion for automation, open source and the CNCF ecosystem. Today I build and run Kubernetes-based platforms with industry best practices.

person.name: Alexandre Astolpho Thomaz
person.bio: Containers strategist, defining future of platforms using kubernetes. Former Reliability Community Technical Leader, responsible for SRE and Quality Assurance strategies.

person.name: Jérôme Masson
person.bio: I began my career as a consultant, specializing in helping clients transition to digital platforms. My journey into cloud-native technologies started in 2017 with Docker and Kubernetes, and has since evolved to include open-source software within the CNCF Landscape. As a consultant with expertise in platform engineering projects, I am passionate about sharing my knowledge at conferences such as Devoxx FR, KCD France, and DevFest Nantes.

person.name: Nurhak Şentürk
person.bio: I’m a cloud-native enthusiast with a focus on Kubernetes, containerization, and microservices. I organize meetups where I share knowledge on Kubernetes, Helm, and CI/CD processes, fostering community engagement. I specialize in helping organizations adopt Kubernetes and improve their DevOps practices through automation and scaling solutions. I also write blog posts on topics like cloud-native trends, best practices, and building scalable applications.

person.name: Taivo Pikkmets
person.bio: Infinitely curious about all things technology, with a special interest in DevOps and Site Reliability Engineering. Passionate about developing my skills and staying current with industry trends and technologies. I love sharing my knowledge and discussing challenging topics to support others in their development and success.

person.name: Yao-Siang Su
person.bio: Yao-Siang Su, a software architect with experience in the Kubernetes ecosystem and distributed systems.  In recent years, he and his team have helped Taiwan's largest job bank website on its journey toward cloud-native. In addition to using CNCF projects such as Kubernetes, Prometheus, Argo CD, Helm, and Istio, he also enjoys using HashiCorp Vault to accomplish various tasks.  In addition to cloud-native technologies, he also enjoys reading and learning the fundamentals of distributed systems and finds great joy in it.

person.name: Aniket Kanade
person.bio: A veteran of tech industry with more than two decades of experience, currently working as DevOps Architect. I've spent most of my tech career in telco industry, while most of my recent work has been in hybrid cloud platform by Azure and on-prem Openshift. Generally, everything kubernetes is my area of ineterest.

person.name: Roy Liang
person.bio: I am a cloud native developer and advocator with over 6 years of experience in cloud native community. I was a cloud native infrastructure SRE and early Kubernetes contributor. I am currently working as a Senior R&D Engineer in Machine Learning Platform team at Tencent. I have participated in multiple cloud native projects and now I am working on cloud native AI infrastructure. I am also a co-author of "Istio Handbook", traslator of "Distributed Machine Learning Pattern" and speaker of KubeCon.

person.name: Steven Yang
person.bio: I’m a security-focused cloud engineer with a background in cybersecurity who became deeply interested in Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies through hands-on work with container platforms. Inspired by the Kubestronaut community, I challenged myself to pursue the CNCF certifications to strengthen both my technical depth and real-world problem-solving skills. My primary interests lie in container and runtime security, Kubernetes hardening, workload identity, and virtualization-based isolation. I’m passionate about advancing research and leadership in cloud-native security and helping organizations build resilient, secure container platforms.

person.name: Mads Skau Nyland
person.bio: Senior Platform Developer who loves Linux and Kubernetes.

person.name: Steffen Petersen
person.bio: Steffen is an experienced DevOps consultant specializing in cloud-native technologies and Kubernetes. He has worked with, and are proficient in, all layers of the stack, ranging from low-level networking all the way to optimizing observability of running workloads. Besides his day-to-day tasks of helping customers with various issues, another part of his job consists of running courses where he teaches cloud-native technologies such as Docker, Kubernetes, Helm and ArgoCD.

person.name: Sun Hyung Choi
person.bio: Technical consultant with a passion for Linux and modern DevOps approaches.

person.name: Victor Laureano
person.bio: I’m a Platform/DevOps Engineer with 7+ years of experience designing, operating, and scaling cloud-native platforms, working primarily on AWS with some projects across Azure and GCP. I currently work hands-on with multi-tenant Kubernetes environments, leading cluster upgrade strategies and supporting critical platform services such as Airflow, n8n, and an Envoy-based API gateway, using Terraform and GitLab CI/CD. KCD Porto in late September 2024 was my first direct interaction with the CNCF community. I’m passionate about platform reliability, security, and enabling engineering teams to build and ship safely at scale.

person.name: Matthieu Rolla
person.bio: I’m a Senior DevOps and Site Reliability Engineer with over a decade of experience in system engineering, automation, and cloud infrastructure. I’ve worked across Europe and the U.S. focusing on reliable and secure on-premise & multi-cloud deployments. Certified across the Kubernetes stack (CKA, CKAD, CKS, KCNA), I enjoy designing practical solutions and building tools that simplify operations.

person.name: Nobuo Enami
person.bio: Nobuo Enami, an engineer at Hitachi, Ltd., has a broad skill set covering both the infrastructure and development aspects of cloud-native systems. With various certifications, application development, and infrastructure knowledge (Kubernetes, multi-cloud), he bridges the gap between development and operations.

person.name: Toru Komatsu
person.bio: Toru contributes to OSS as an individual contributor. He is the author and lead developer of youki, an OCI Runtime in Rust, and I am also a maintainer of the OCI Runtime Specification. Additionally, he serves as a reviewer for runwasi and is involved in developing a world that utilizes containers and Wasm. Lately, he has developed an interest in Kubernetes schedulers. Furthermore, he has given presentations at KubeDay Japan and has spoken at various community events in Japan.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: The area of container runtime is the unsung hero of the Cloud Native space, where the intricacies of the technology and new features often don't get the spotlight they deserve. As a developer in this field, I want to help more people become aware of the exciting features it has to offer. I aim to encourage them to take the leap into this fascinating domain! I am also looking forward to assisting the Japanese Cloud Native community in becoming more vibrant on an international level.

person.name: Marit Nodland Lund
person.bio: Senior infrastructure engineer and cloud enthusiast. Excited to work more with the CNCF ecosystem and participate in the community.

person.name: Sébastian Voutat
person.bio: I am a Site Reliability Engineer with a strong focus on Kubernetes, automation, and CNCF technologies. I regularly attend cloud-native conferences such as KubeCon, CloudNativeCon Europe, and local meetups in Zurich. I am passionate about improving system reliability and enjoy collaborating with DevOps and architecture teams to drive innovation.

person.name: Andres Perez Velez
person.bio: I’m a Senior Technical Specialist at Microsoft. I am passionate about Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. My role is to help customers design and implement cloud-ready architectures.

person.name: Anurag Kumar
person.bio: I'm contributor and maintainer of KubeArmor (A CNCF runtime security project). I'm interested in Kubernetes Security. You can read my blogs here https://kranurag.devWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm excited to be a CNCF ambassador because I'll try to bring and educate more people towards CNCF ecosystem. CNCF is one of the most welcoming community. I also want to share my knowledge when it comes to Kubernetes and other CNCF projects to new peoples who're getting started. Finally, I would love to meet and connect with fellow CNCF ambassadors.

person.name: Mohit Pant
person.bio: I’m a DevOps Lead and cloud-native professional specializing in designing, automating, and operating scalable Kubernetes platforms. My focus areas include DevOps and SRE practices, CI/CD automation with Jenkins and ArgoCD, and building reliable production systems on AWS and Azure using Terraform and Ansible. I have extensive experience with observability and monitoring stacks such as Prometheus, Grafana, Zabbix, and Datadog to ensure platform resilience and performance. As a mentor, I enjoy guiding engineers on cloud-native and Kubernetes journeys—connect with me on LinkedIn if you’re aiming to get guidance on becoming a Kubestronaut.

person.name: Sourav Bera
person.bio: • Solution Architect at Microsoft, specialized in AKS and GKE. • Linux Certified. • Community Speaker, Judge, and Mentor. • Organizes and speaks at CNCG Chapter (Delhi, Gurugram, Noida), Azure Developer Community, and Microsoft Reactor Program. • Expert in Managed Kubernetes Services. • Researcher and Hackathon Advocate:  https://www.linkedin.com/in/zeo-shark/details/publications/ • Active on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/zeo-shark/

person.name: Vishwajeet Mishra
person.bio: I'm a Lead Project Engineer and a DevOps professional, deeply enthusiastic about cloud-native technologies. When I'm not immersed in the world of engineering, you'll find me exploring beautiful, unexpected places on Earth, often with a soundtrack of my favorite music, a song to sing, or simply enjoying a happy time with my family.

person.name: Wouter Damman
person.bio: Hi! I’m Wouter, a Cloud Engineer from the Netherlands with a passion for Kubernetes and the cloud-native world. My interest in technology and automation led me down this path, allowing me to explore how the cloud-native world fits together. both at work and in my homelab. I enjoy solving problems through automation and turning ideas into reliable, maintainable solutions.
